description: Add the comprehensive Temporal Specificity in Lyrics section across all 7 files in the songwriting-kb repository, with full academic citations, tables, and practical guidelines.
steps: Step 1: Edit core/methodology/songwriting.md - Insert a new section '## Temporal Specificity in Lyrics (Times, Dates, Numbers)' BEFORE the '## Core Principles' section (which starts around line 400). This is the canonical source. Include: (a) The core tension between Pattison (specific=universal via sensory memory) and Murphey (vague=universal via appropriability, 94% of hits avoid time specifics), with full citations. (b) The synthesis statement: 'Exact time/date/number language is most effective when it behaves as deictic scene-work plus prosodic design, and least effective when it functions as unearned information.' (c) The 6 Functions table (Emotional Anchor, Narrative Urgency, Hook/Memorability, Cinematic Specificity, Cultural Code, Structural Device - with examples like '3am', '25 or 6 to 4', '9 to 5', 'At seventeen', 'Summer of 69/Friday night', '7 Years'). (d) The Oddness Effect with caveat (irregular numbers signal authenticity via precision anchoring, BUT precision that reads as cleverness-display reverses the effect). (e) Days-of-week cultural loading table (Monday=dread, Friday=freedom, Saturday=celebration, Sunday=melancholy, Tue/Wed/Thu=neutral-use only when ordinariness IS the point). (f) When to Use vs When to Avoid lists. (g) Practical test: 'Can the listener FEEL the number on first listen?'. (h) Resolution of the tension: hybrid approach (specific verses for grounding + vague choruses for universality). Cite: Pattison 2009, Murphey 1989/1992, Werner, Perricone 2018, Wilson 2025, Jenkins 2018, Stockwell 2002, Gavins, Voice & Whiteley, Janiszewski & Uy, Mason et al., NSAI.

Step 2: Edit SONGWRITING_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md - Add a subsection '### Temporal Specificity (Times, Dates, Numbers)' under Section 6 (Emotional Arc & Storytelling), after the existing 'Narrative Techniques' content (around line 230). Keep concise (30-50 lines). Include: the 6 functions as a compact table, the Pattison/Murphey tension in 2-3 sentences, days-of-week loading as a compact table, practical guidelines (use when/avoid when as bullet lists), reference back to core/methodology/songwriting.md for full treatment.

Step 3: Edit MUSIC_PRODUCTION_THEORY.md - Add a new discipline section '### 13. COGNITIVE POETICS & DEIXIS -- How Language Positions the Listener' after section 12 (Computational Musicology/MIR, which ends around line 320). Include sources: Stockwell (Cognitive Poetics 2002) on temporal deixis and deictic shift theory; Gavins (Text World Theory) on temporal parameters as world-building elements; Voice & Whiteley on multimodal deixis in song (3 levels: discourse-world anchoring + meter/prosody interaction + attention competition); Werner on register analysis of pop lyrics (simulating immediacy while being planned/performed/distant); Murphey (1989, 1992) on corpus findings (94% no time of enunciation, 80% no place, ZERO precise dates); Wilson (2025) on corpus stylistics (1291-song UK #1 corpus, Buhler's deixis am phantasma); Kreyer & Mukherjee on pop lyrics sitting uneasily between speech and writing. Format as a table of scholars/contributions plus a 'Production Questions This Answers' section and 'Key Concepts' table matching the style of sections 1-12.

Step 4: Edit references/SUNO_STYLE_GENRE_REFERENCE.md - Add a new section '## 13. TEMPORAL SPECIFICITY IN SUNO RENDERING' at the end of the file (after the Suno Version Status table). Keep it 10-15 lines. Cover: numbers are rendered literally by Suno, days of week can affect mood interpretation in the generated audio, precise times in lyrics help Suno anchor production choices when referenced in Production Direction, cultural-code times (Friday night, 3am) carry implicit genre/mood signals that Suno recognizes.

Step 5: Edit references/CRITIQUE_REFERENCE.md - Add a new row to the COMMON FLAG PATTERNS table (at line ~445, after the existing 'Undeclared layer' row): '| Unearned temporal specificity | Time/date that does not anchor, ground, or hook -- just conveys information | Replace with culturally-loaded alternative OR cut |'. Also add a brief note (2-3 lines) above the table or as a footnote referencing the full temporal specificity guidelines in core/methodology/songwriting.md.

Step 6: Edit CREDITS.md - Add all new academic sources to the Academic Sources table. New entries: | **Tim Murphey** (1989, 1992) | Pop lyric corpus analysis -- temporal vagueness and 'appropriability' |, | **Werner** (register analysis) | Pop lyrics as simulated immediacy -- planned performance of conversational register |, | **Jack Perricone** (*Great Songwriting Techniques*, 2018) | Scenario development -- time grounds scene, prosodic interaction |, | **Rebecca Wilson** (PhD, 2025) | 1291-song corpus stylistics, imaginative deixis in popular lyrics |, | **SD Jenkins** (Research Master, 2018) | Form, content, craft -- verse/chorus as rearrangement of time/space |, | **Peter Stockwell** (*Cognitive Poetics*, 2002) | Temporal deixis, deictic shift theory, deictic centre |, | **Joanna Gavins** (*Text World Theory*) | Temporal parameters as world-building elements |, | **Voice & Whiteley** | Multimodal cognitive analysis of deixis in song |, | **Janiszewski & Uy** (precision anchoring) | Precise numbers anchor more strongly due to finer-resolution scale |, | **Mason, Lee, Wiley, Ames** | Precise offers perceived as more informed -- precision signals expertise |, | **Kreyer & Mukherjee** (corpus linguistics) | Pop lyrics on boundary between speech and writing |, | **NSAI** (Nashville Songwriters Association) | 'Be descriptive, but not so specific that your audience cannot relate' |. Place these alphabetically or at the end of the existing table.

Step 7: Edit CHANGELOG.md - Add under ## [Unreleased] ### Added: '- Temporal Specificity guideline (times, dates, numbers in lyrics) added to songwriting methodology, knowledge base, production theory, critique reference, and Suno rendering guide -- backed by 13 academic/professional sources (Pattison, Murphey, Werner, Perricone, Wilson, Jenkins, Stockwell, Gavins, Voice & Whiteley, Janiszewski & Uy, Mason et al., Kreyer & Mukherjee, NSAI)'
acceptance_criteria: core/methodology/songwriting.md contains a complete 'Temporal Specificity in Lyrics' section with all 6 functions, the synthesis statement, oddness effect, days-of-week table, use/avoid guidelines, and practical test
SONGWRITING_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md contains a concise (30-50 line) subsection under Section 6 with the 6 functions table and key guidelines
MUSIC_PRODUCTION_THEORY.md contains a new section 13 on Cognitive Poetics & Deixis with all 7 scholar entries formatted consistently with existing sections 1-12
references/SUNO_STYLE_GENRE_REFERENCE.md has a 10-15 line practical note about temporal specificity in Suno rendering
references/CRITIQUE_REFERENCE.md COMMON FLAG PATTERNS table has the new 'Unearned temporal specificity' row
CREDITS.md Academic Sources table contains all 12 new source entries
CHANGELOG.md has the temporal specificity entry under [Unreleased] Added
All citations are consistent across files (same author names, same years, same title formatting)
The synthesis statement appears in full only in core/methodology/songwriting.md; other files reference or summarize it
The full 6-function table appears only in core/methodology/songwriting.md; SONGWRITING_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md has a compact version; other files reference it
verification: Run: grep -c 'Temporal Specificity' /projects/sandbox/songwriting-kb/core/methodology/songwriting.md /projects/sandbox/songwriting-kb/SONGWRITING_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md /projects/sandbox/songwriting-kb/MUSIC_PRODUCTION_THEORY.md /projects/sandbox/songwriting-kb/references/SUNO_STYLE_GENRE_REFERENCE.md /projects/sandbox/songwriting-kb/references/CRITIQUE_REFERENCE.md -- all should return >= 1
Run: grep 'Temporal Specificity' /projects/sandbox/songwriting-kb/CHANGELOG.md -- should return the changelog entry
Run: wc -l on all modified files to confirm content was added (not deleted)
Visual review: confirm markdown table formatting is valid (consistent pipe separators, header rows)
findings: All 7 files modified successfully. 165 lines added across the codebase. All verification grep checks pass. Commit on main branch. Review feedback addressed in commit 1cad001: (1) CREDITS.md new entries reorganized from alphabetical to thematic grouping matching pre-existing convention, (2) Gavins citation updated to include full title and year 2007 across CREDITS.md and MUSIC_PRODUCTION_THEORY.md, (3) Perricone sort issue resolved by thematic grouping, (4) Suno rendering claims softened with qualifiers and confidence note added.
